claims_verified.claim: Round-4 fix: genuine OS write failures are wrapped into path-free WriteError at both create and write time
claims_verified.evidence: note_server/writer.py:71-75 (os.open OSError -> WriteError 'could not create the note file') and 79-80 (fdopen/write OSError -> WriteError 'could not write the note contents'); neither message interpolates the path

claims_verified.claim: Prior blocker resolved end-to-end: a write into a read-only folder no longer leaks the path
claims_verified.evidence: Reproduced the exact prior-leak scenario (chmod 0o500 folder) through FastMCP call_tool: RESULT text 'Error: write failed - the note could not be saved.'; PATH LEAKED: False

claims_verified.claim: AC6 write-error path now has covering tests at writer, handler, and end-to-end level
claims_verified.evidence: test_writer.py:60-92 (test_write_note_wraps_os_error_as_path_free_write_error, test_write_note_wraps_write_time_os_error); test_server.py:108-144 (test_write_error_response_is_clean_and_hides_folder_path asserts 'write' in result and folder path absent; test_call_tool_write_failure_never_leaks_path asserts no leak via call_tool)

claims_verified.claim: Full suite, ruff, and basedpyright all pass
claims_verified.evidence: pytest: 712 passed in 85.28s; note_server subset: 54 passed; ruff: All checks passed!; basedpyright: 0 errors, 1571 warnings (errors are the gate)

claims_verified.claim: TOTP is standard RFC 6238 and the hardcoded base32 secret decodes correctly
claims_verified.evidence: Independent recompute: KNVWKZLWFVHWW2LOMF3WC -> b'Skeev-Okinawa'; codes for ts 0/59/1234567890 = 411400/795119/948172 match test_totp.py:34-37

claims_verified.claim: verify() accepts current & ±1 window, rejects ±2/wrong/non-ascii, skips negative counter
project_name guard rejects empty/dot/slash/traversal/whitespace/backslash and writes nothing
No-clobber dedup via O_CREAT|O_EXCL yields name-2.md/name-3.md and preserves original
Config env vars all required with no default; missing folder/host/port fail at startup
Exactly one save_note tool with three required string params is registered
claims_verified.evidence: test_server.py:28-35 asserts [t.name]==['save_note'], required=={token,project_name,content}, all string; passes

claims_verified.claim: mcp is the only runtime dep and basedpyright include covers note_server

test_assessment: All acceptance criteria are covered. AC1 (tool surface) by test_registers_exactly_one_save_note_tool; AC2 (TOTP accept/reject + no-write-on-reject + reference vector) by test_totp.py:45-77 and test_server.py:65-79, independently cross-checked; AC3 (project_name guard incl. traversal) by test_writer.py:20-26 and test_server.py:85-93; AC4 (happy-path verbatim write) by test_happy_path_writes_verbatim_and_returns_filename; AC5 (no-clobber dedup) by test_writer.py:34-45 and test_dedup_returns_new_name_and_keeps_original; AC6 (no path leak across auth/validation/write-error) now fully covered — the previously-missing write-error branch is exercised at writer level (test_write_note_wraps_os_error_as_path_free_write_error, test_write_note_wraps_write_time_os_error), handler level (test_write_error_response_is_clean_and_hides_folder_path), and end-to-end (test_call_tool_write_failure_never_leaks_path); AC7 (config via env) by test_note_config.py; AC8 (green suite + dep hygiene) verified. Every new function (decode_secret, totp, verify, validate_project_name, write_note, from_env, handle_save_note) now has failure-mode tests including error paths, boundary/empty inputs, adversarial names, and OS-failure branches. My prior blocker (unwrapped OSError leaking the absolute folder path) is fully resolved and regression-tested.
residual_risks: config.py validates the folder is an existing dir but not that it is writable; a non-writable folder now yields a clean, path-free write error at request time rather than a startup failure — acceptable and covered.
The path-escape defence-in-depth branch (writer.py:64-65) remains unreachable via the public API given the regex, so it stays inherently untestable without bypassing validation — acceptable.
